entries.context: The most common Japanese insult. Used in arguments, anime, and everyday frustration. Severity depends on tone.
entries.suggested_response: Say 'Sumimasen' (Excuse me / I'm sorry) and bow slightly.

entries.context: Common in Osaka and western Japan. In Tokyo, more offensive than in Kansai where it can be affectionate.
Tells someone to disappear. Rude and dismissive — signaling they don't want you around.
entries.suggested_response: Calmly move away. Say 'Sumimasen, ikimashou' (Sorry, let me go).

entries.context: Extremely offensive. Telling someone to die. Rarely used in person but serious when it is.
entries.suggested_response: Leave immediately. Contact authorities if you feel threatened.

entries.context: Accusation of theft. Japan is very low-crime — being accused is serious and can attract attention.
entries.suggested_response: Stay calm. Show belongings. Get a store employee or police to mediate.

entries.context: Often an exclamation of frustration, not always directed at a person. Equivalent to 'damn it'.
entries.suggested_response: May not be directed at you. Observe context before reacting.
